description_long: This is a light twin-engine airplane with a streamlined fuselage and a glazed cockpit canopy. The wings are straight and mounted at a mid-level on the fuselage, extending symmetrically with visible propellers on each engine nacelle. The tail section features a conventional horizontal stabilizer and twin vertical stabilizers, giving it a distinctive H-tail configuration. The landing gear appears to be a tricycle type, with the main gear under the wings and a single nose wheel. The exterior is primarily painted white, with minimal markings except for a large number '7' on the tail fin. The propeller hubs are highlighted in red, and the cockpit windows are rendered in a light blue tint.
description: A white, twin-propeller airplane with a glazed cockpit, H-tail, and tricycle landing gear. Features include mid-mounted straight wings and red-tipped propellers.
description_view_0: Front view showing the twin propellers, glazed cockpit, and symmetrical mid-mounted wings.
description_view_1: Angled side view highlighting the cockpit canopy, engine nacelles, and the number '7' on the tail fin.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the H-tail configuration with twin vertical stabilizers and the horizontal stabilizer.
description_view_3: Opposite angled side view, again showing the cockpit, propellers, and tail number, with more emphasis on the right wing.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Mid-wing, straight wings with no visible winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Twin piston engines mounted on the wings, each with a visible propeller.
attribute_tail_design: H-tail configuration with twin vertical stabilizers and a single horizontal stabilizer.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Tricycle landing gear with two main gear units under the wings and a single nose gear.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with a multi-panel glazed canopy.
